Is Trump Tuned Out to Americans’ Financial Worries? A Remark Suggests Yes.
Asked if he was motivated by Americans’ financial woes to make a deal to end the war with Iran, President Trump responded, “Not even a little bit.”
The comment from President Trump was fraught with political risk: “I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation.”
His remark came in response to a question about whether he was motivated by Americans’ financial situation to try to end the war with Iran.
“Not even a little bit,” he said, saying he was focused on stopping Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon.
The response flouted fundamental rules of American politics — that voters are driven by economic concerns and dislike politicians who don’t seem to care about them.
Trump is facing a politically worrisome moment. Prices are rising, his approval ratings are down and his party’s midterm fortunes may ride on him.
